channel.title: Pratik Dhanave — Blog
channel.description: Long-form writing on multi-agent AI, medical AI governance, HIPAA-aware architecture, and cloud-native systems.

item.title: Hosting and the Capstone App — MAF in Go
item.description: Wrap Go agents in A2A, MCP, and AG-UI transports, then run the end-to-end client/server capstone where a host agent uses remote specialists as tools.

item.title: Advanced Workflows — MAF in Go
item.description: Durable MAF workflows in Go: checkpoint and rehydrate a fresh graph, pause on a RequestPort for a human, nest sub-workflows, and coordinate via scoped shared state.

item.title: Orchestration Patterns — MAF in Go
item.description: The Sequential, Concurrent, and Group Chat orchestration builders in agent-framework-go, plus wrapping a whole workflow as one nestable agent.

item.title: Workflows with Agents — MAF in Go
item.description: Agents as Go workflow executors: switch routing, fan-out and fan-in barriers, and mixing typed function nodes with agent nodes in one graph.

item.title: Workflow Mechanics — MAF in Go
item.description: The MAF workflow model in Go: executors bound to IDs, AddEdge wiring, WithOutputFrom, and typed WatchStream events - plus an upstream route-builder fix.

item.title: Observability, Safety, and Providers — MAF in Go
item.description: Wrap every MAF run in an OpenTelemetry span, gate risky tool actions behind a permission handler, and swap Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Copilot, and Azure behind one agent.

item.title: Middleware — MAF in Go
item.description: A guardrail middleware that blocks an MAF Go run before the model, chained ahead of a logger — plus the tool-approval nil-update panic I fixed upstream in PR #472.

item.title: Shaping a Run — MAF in Go
item.description: Decode typed structs from a run, drain a streamed response by ranging it, and send an image with RunMessage and DataContent.

item.title: Conversation and Memory — MAF in Go
item.description: A Session threads history into each run and a ContextProvider carries memory across sessions. Because a Session is JSON, both survive a process restart.

item.title: Giving an Agent Tools — MAF in Go
item.description: Wrap a typed Go function as a tool with functool.MustNew, let the model call it, and compose whole agents as tools with agenttool.New.

item.title: Your First Agent — MAF in Go
item.description: The minimal MAF loop in Go: a foundryprovider agent, DefaultAzureCredential, and one RunText you either Collect or range over as a Go 1.23 iterator.

item.title: Learning the Microsoft Agent Framework in Go
item.description: I learned the whole Microsoft Agent Framework in Go by building one runnable lesson per concept against Azure AI Foundry. Here is the 12-track map.

item.title: Hosting and the Capstone App — MAF in Python
item.description: Host MAF agents with DevUI, A2A, MCP, and AG-UI, then build DocQA — a grounded, cited multi-agent app that ties the whole Python series together.

item.title: Advanced Workflows — MAF in Python
item.description: Durable MAF workflows in Python: checkpoint and resume every superstep, suspend on request_info for a human decision, and package a workflow as an agent.

item.title: Orchestration Patterns — MAF in Python
item.description: Sequential, Concurrent, Group Chat, Handoff, Magentic — the five prebuilt MAF orchestrations in Python and when each beats hand-wiring a graph.

item.title: Workflows with Agents — MAF in Python
item.description: Agents are just workflow executors: switch-case routing, fan-out/fan-in concurrency, and mixing plain function nodes with agent nodes in one graph.

item.title: Workflow Mechanics — MAF in Python
item.description: The MAF workflow model in Python: executors as nodes, edges as data flow, switch-case routing, and typed streaming events - learned model-free.

item.title: Observability, Safety, and Providers — MAF in Python
item.description: Turn MAF agent runs into OpenTelemetry spans, block prompt injection with information-flow control, and swap model providers behind one Agent API.

item.title: Middleware — MAF in Python
item.description: Wrapping an MAF agent run in Python with async middleware seams — timing, logging, and a guardrail that short-circuits a tool call before it runs.

item.title: Shaping a Run — MAF in Python
item.description: Three dials on one run() call: typed results via response_format, consuming a stream event by event, and sending an image alongside text.

item.title: Conversation and Memory — MAF in Python
item.description: MAF agents are stateless. Sessions carry one conversation; context providers carry memory across all of them. Here is the mental model in real code.

item.title: Giving an Agent Tools — MAF in Python
item.description: Turn a plain Python function into a tool the model can call. The @tool decorator, the tool-call loop, multiple tools, and what the model actually sees.

item.title: Your First Agent — MAF in Python
item.description: The minimal MAF loop in Python: a FoundryChatClient, an Agent whose instructions are its whole personality, run non-streaming and streaming.

item.title: Learning the Microsoft Agent Framework in Python
item.description: I learned the whole Microsoft Agent Framework in Python by building one runnable lesson per concept against Azure AI Foundry. Here is the 12-track map.

item.title: AI-Driven Observability for Trustworthy Agentic AI
item.description: Agents return a clean 200 OK and still be wrong, unsafe, or expensive. Why agentic AI needs a new observability layer — LLM-as-judge, safety metrics, and the four lifecycle stages.

item.title: Governance with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description: OWASP Agentic Top 10 coverage with YAML policy files, two API surfaces, and a metric bridge that shows policy denials in Grafana.

item.title: Refactor: from rolled-our-own to MAF-native
item.description: I built memory, communication, security, governance, and evals from scratch first. Then I deleted most of it and used the Microsoft Agent Framework-native packages. Here is the audit table and what survived.

item.title: Multi-turn evals from first principles
item.description: Single-turn evals check one decision. Multi-turn evals check the whole trajectory. A Python harness with three evaluators, an offline test suite, and the judge prompt that actually works.

item.title: Observability for multi-agent: traces and metrics
item.description: OpenTelemetry through the Microsoft Agent Framework's configure_otel_providers, custom workflow spans, custom metrics for runs/duration/agent selection, Jaeger + Prometheus + Grafana wiring, and the set-once latch gotcha.

item.title: A2A: when the workflow IS the broker
item.description: The reference architecture distinguishes request-based and message-driven agent communication. For in-process orchestration, the workflow IS the broker — and A2A is just the wire format.

item.title: Memory done right with MAF
item.description: AgentSession is short-term memory. MemoryContextProvider + MemoryFileStore is long-term memory. Mem0 is long-term memory for serious workloads. The boundary that matters and how to implement each.

item.title: Agent registry as a project-local convention
item.description: The Microsoft Agent Framework deliberately does not ship an agent registry. Here is why that is the right call, and what to build as a project-local convention when you need one.

item.title: Ollama as the default LLM for enterprise-shaped systems
item.description: PROVIDER=ollama, granite4.1:3b, zero API keys, no Azure account. How to make a multi-agent project that demonstrates enterprise patterns without requiring enterprise infrastructure.

item.title: Five MAF orchestration shapes — adding Group Chat and...
item.description: The first ten posts treated the Microsoft Agent Framework as having four orchestration patterns. The official docs say five. Here are the two I missed — Group Chat and Magentic — and why they matter.

item.title: Four orchestration patterns in MAF — and when to pick each
item.description: Sequential, Concurrent, Handoff, and Custom WorkflowBuilder. Four shapes the Microsoft Agent Framework ships out of the box, when to pick each, and the gotchas that cost me a day.

item.title: The 12-chapter reference architecture, in 102 Python files
item.description: Microsoft published a 12-chapter reference architecture for multi-agent systems and a separate framework — the Microsoft Agent Framework — to build them. Here is what the 102 Python files actually contain and how they map to the chapters.

item.title: Lessons from Converting 18 Agents in 90 Days
item.description: What worked, what was hard, and what we'd do differently. Real numbers: 18 agents, 90 days, 5 governance policies, 4 provider swaps.

item.title: Deployment & A2A: From ADK's Web Deploy to Microsoft Agent Framework's Scalable Architecture
item.description: Deploy Microsoft Agent Framework multi-agent systems on Cloud Run with A2A agent-to-agent communication, autoscaling, load balancing, and production observability dashboards.

item.title: Callbacks and Middleware: Porting ADK Hooks to Microsoft Agent Framework's Decorator Pattern
item.description: Migrate ADK callbacks to Microsoft Agent Framework composable middleware: decorators for audit logging, retry with backoff, token budget enforcement, and OpenTelemetry tracing.

item.title: Provider Abstraction: From Gemini-Only to Swappable LLMs
item.description: Zero-code LLM provider swaps across environments: Ollama for dev, OpenAI for staging, Azure Foundry for prod. Same agents, different models.

item.title: Tool Wrapping: From ADK Functions to Microsoft Agent Framework Governed Tools
item.description: Migrate ADK functions to Microsoft Agent Framework governed tools with policy enforcement, DLP scanning, approval gates, and OPA integration for production agent systems.

item.title: Token Exchange Patterns: Porting Multi-Turn State from ADK to Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: Sessions to threads: porting multi-turn state from ADK to Microsoft Agent Framework. Token budgeting, long-term memory, and conversation audit trails.

item.title: The Executor Pattern: ADK→Microsoft Agent Framework Conversion for Agentic Control Flow
item.description: How to port ADK's orchestration callbacks to Microsoft Agent Framework builders without losing control. The executor pattern: you own the loop.

item.title: Why We Migrated from Google ADK to Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: The philosophy, trade-offs, and what we learned converting 18+ agents in 3 months. Provider abstraction as the foundation for portable agents.

item.title: OpenTelemetry and Evaluation in Multi-Agent Workflows —...
item.description: How to instrument multi-agent systems with OpenTelemetry, propagate trace context across an in-memory bus, and build a layered evaluation pipeline — from real-time policy gates to async LLM-as-judge to SLO-based trust scoring. Everything I learned building Genie.

item.title: Running a HIPAA-Aware Multi-Agent Medical AI on GKE: A...
item.description: Google's GKE AI infrastructure docs list ~40 integrations. Here's a field map of which ones actually matter when the workload is a HIPAA-aware multi-agent medical AI, and where the gaps sit.

item.title: The Right-to-Explanation Handler: GDPR Article 22 as a...
item.description: Build a GDPR Article 22 compliant explanation endpoint in Go that turns audit logs and eval stores into regulator-friendly answers for AI decisions.

item.title: Time-bound privileged access — building the PCSE §1.4...
item.description: Request → N-eyes approve → window-of-time → automatic expiry, with every transition written to a hash-chained audit log. The package that closes Gap #1 from the PCSE map.

item.title: HL7 v2 in 2026: Why This 50-Year-Old Protocol Still...
item.description: Why HL7 v2 — a 50-year-old pipe-delimited protocol — still drives most US hospital ADT integrations in 2026, and what a clean Go parser looks like in ~300 lines.

item.title: Studying for the AIGP? Here's a Reference Implementation...
item.description: Studying for the IAPP AI Governance Professional credential? Here's an open-source Go codebase that demonstrates ~70% of the body of knowledge in working code.
